paper.title: A systematic method to identify runaways from star clusters produced from single-binary interactions: A case study of M67

extraction.summary: The paper identifies one runaway star-runaway binary pair ejected from the open cluster M67, but does not present any object as possibly unbound from the Milky Way. All candidates are cluster escapers/runaways with velocities measured relative to the cluster center-of-mass, not Galactic-unbound candidates.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR3 catalog providing 5D kinematic data (positions, proper motions, parallaxes) for stars in a 150 pc field of view around M67.
Preliminary cuts in distance (cylinder 300 pc diameter/length centered on M67), position (outside tidal radius of 17 pc), and minimum 2D velocity relative to cluster COM (>2.6 km/s escape velocity).
Transform proper motions to velocities relative to M67 center-of-mass motion using orthographic projection and perspective expansion corrections.

source_refs.context: Velocity transformation and traceback position calculation

method_chain.summary: Apply traceback space/time coincidence, 180-degree angle of intersection, velocity ratio >2 with brightness consistency, and CMD position relative to M67 isochrone.
Identify the most probable RS-RB pair consistent with triple disintegration from single-binary interactions in M67.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The single runaway star-runaway binary pair identified by the paper as ejected from the open cluster M67 via single-binary interaction.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as runaway escapers from the open cluster M67, with velocities measured relative to the cluster center-of-mass. The paper does not assess their Galactocentric kinematics or present them as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential. They are ordinary cluster runaways, not Galactic-unbound HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: Results section describing the single RS-RB pair candidate ejected from M67, including Gaia source IDs and velocities relative to cluster COM
